title: AEP RubricScore Claim
description: v1.0.3.1 A4 backport. RubricScore is a new claim.type value formally added to claims.jsonl row vocab. Every rubric verdict's per-dimension score becomes a structured queryable claim instead of prose narrative. The rubric_definitional_closure_set field is the LOAD-BEARING addition that closes the v1.0.3 VG04 root cause (list-valued recall-field overlap-threshold ambiguity). Composes_with: doctrine/02-truth-tags.html, doctrine/41-hash-chained-receipt-ledger.html, doctrine/69-verification-law-and-operator-spec-sovereignty.html sec69.5 operator-verbatim-sacred, AEP_v1_0_3_SPEC.md sec3 RegexicalCue, rater_quorum_attestation.schema.json.

type.description: Discriminator. Always 'RubricScore'. Adds 'RubricScore' to the v0.5+ claim.type vocab.

rubric_id.description: Stable id of the rubric this score applies to (e.g., 'vg04-blind-recall-v1.0.3' or 'vg04-blind-recall-v1.0.3.1').

rubric_version.description: Optional version of the rubric. Allows tracking rubric refinement across v1.0.3 -> v1.0.3.1.

dimension_id.description: Stable id of the dimension being scored (e.g., 'failure_prevented_overlap', 'packet_id_drift', 'stop_condition_present', 'fabrication_count', 'overall_rubric_5pt').

score.description: Numeric score. Range determined by score_scale.

score_scale.description: Scale identifier. 0_to_5 default for VG04 rubric (matches operator source.md L274-280).

score_label.description: Optional categorical label corresponding to operator source.md L274-280 rubric levels.

bound_to_artifact_sha256.description: Anchor: sha256 of the artifact being scored (e.g., recall_payload, packet, attempt). Drift here invalidates the score.

rater_principal_id.description: Who scored. Must match a raters[].principal_id in the associated RaterQuorumAttestation.

rater_role.description: Optional rater role for lens-class queries.

rationale_sha256.description: sha256 of the rationale prose. Allows audit without inlining full text.

rationale_text.description: Optional inline rationale. If present and rationale_sha256 also present, validator verifies sha256(rationale_text) == rationale_sha256.

session_id.description: Session in which this score was emitted. Validates against RaterQuorumAttestation session_id uniqueness.

rubric_definitional_closure_set.description: LOAD-BEARING ADDITION: closes the v1.0.3 VG04 rubric-definitional-gap on list-valued recall fields. Every dimension that is scored MUST have a closure entry specifying: (a) how partial-overlap is resolved, (b) the partial-credit formula, (c) the list-overlap threshold (for list-valued dimensions), and (d) the load-bearing-vs-decorative classifier. This is the field that makes the rubric MACHINE-CHECKABLE rather than rater-discretion.

dimension_id.description: Must match a dimension_id used in a RubricScore claim for this rubric.

definitional_resolution.description: Plain-English statement of how this dimension is resolved when ambiguous. Example for failure_prevented[]: 'Item is LOAD-BEARING if it names a specific lesson_id or doctrine sec-number; DECORATIVE otherwise. Score gold-overlap on LOAD-BEARING items only.'

partial_credit_formula.description: Computable formula expressing partial credit. Example: 'partial_credit = (overlap_count_load_bearing / gold_load_bearing_count) clamped 0-1; final_score = partial_credit * 4.0 + (presence_of_stop_condition * 1.0)'. Validator REPRODUCES this computation if all inputs are present in the bound artifact.

list_overlap_threshold.description: For list-valued dimensions: fraction of gold-list items that must overlap for the field to count as 'present' rather than 'missing'. Default 0.5 (majority overlap) per v1.0.3 judge tiebreaker analysis.

load_bearing_classifier.description: Decision rule that distinguishes load-bearing from decorative items in a list. Example: 'item is load-bearing if substring contains "lesson_id" OR "sec-" OR matches /\bsec\d+/i; else decorative.'

applies_to_scale.description: Which score_scale this closure entry applies to.

version.description: Optional version of this closure rule (e.g., '1.0.3.1.a' for the first revision).

computed_inputs.description: Inputs the partial_credit_formula references, captured for audit reproducibility. Example: {gold_load_bearing_count: 3, overlap_count_load_bearing: 1, presence_of_stop_condition: 1}.

computed_score_from_formula.description: Score recomputed by validator from partial_credit_formula + computed_inputs. Validator emits RUBRICSCORE_FORMULA_DRIFT warning if |score - computed_score_from_formula| > 0.25.

rater_quorum_attestation_id.description: Optional pointer to the RaterQuorumAttestation that this score participates in (forming the multi-rater bundle).
